system_prompt: You are a knowledgeable research assistant. Answer from your own knowledge. Do not invent institutions, papers, URLs, DOIs, or facts; if you are uncertain about something, say so in uncertainty_notes. Return valid JSON exactly in the format requested.
user_prompt:
List the 15 most influential research papers in software engineering. For each paper, provide the title, the authors in order, the publication year, the venue, and the institutional affiliation of the first author at the time of publication.

Return JSON with:
- papers: list of objects with rank, title, authors, year, venue, first_author_affiliation
- uncertainty_notes

  "uncertainty_notes": "Ranking highly influential papers is inherently subjective, but these entries are widely regarded as classics and some of the most heavily cited within Software Engineering literature. A few minor technical points: 1) Ernst et al.'s paper was first published at ICSE 1999 under the title 'Dynamically Discovering Likely Program Invariants'; the 2001 journal version provided is the one with the extended title. 2) David Parnas was affiliated with the University of Victoria around the 1986 publication of his 'Rational Design Process' paper, though he also maintained deep ties with the Naval Research Laboratory (NRL). 3) Barry Boehm developed the Spiral Model while at TRW Defense Systems Group, and the affiliation on the 1988 publication is credited as such, prior to his move to USC. 4) Mark Weiser's 'Program Slicing' was initially presented at ICSE in 1981, but the 1984 IEEE TSE journal article is frequently cited in its place."
}
